title: Bridge Essay: Baseline functional connectivity of the basal forebrain-cortical circuit predict

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Body-energy** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Phase Body-energy bridge-v3 targeted acquisition.

**Baseline functional connectivity of the basal forebrain-cortical circuit predict taVNS treatment response in primary insomnia: arandomized controlled trial and fMRI study.** — BMC medicine (2025). PMID: 40629377. DOI: 10.1186/s12916-025-04126-7. Score: 44. Dysfunctional basal forebrain (BF) connectivity contributes to primary insomnia (PI). This study investigated whether transcutaneous auricular vagus nerve stimulation (taVNS) modulates BF functional connectivity (FC) in patients with PI and whether baseline FC predicts taVNS treatment response. Seventy patients with PI were randomized to real or sham taVNS for 4 weeks. Clinical assessments-including Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index (PSQI], Insomnia Severity Index (ISI] and Zung's Self-Rating Anxie
